Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for the three remediation tickets using linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or query: "duplicate receipt" / "invoice" / "CloudWatch" or team: "team_engineering"). Finds ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, state_todo, Dmitri Volkov), ENG-225 (issue_aws_cost_alerting, state_in_progress, Anh Nguyễn), and ENG-230 (issue_invoice_attachment_safety, state_in_progress, Lena Björkström, P1).
OE 2: Read comments on ENG-220 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection"). Discovers Dmitri's April 15 comment: hash matching is done, but PDF similarity scoring has not started. Discovers Samira's April 16 comment: Lena will take on the PDF scorer once ENG-230 ships, meaning ENG-220 is blocked on ENG-230.
OE 3: Read comments on ENG-225 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_aws_cost_alerting"). Discovers Anh's April 12 comment: staging deployed, needs production validation with Alejandro. Discovers Anh's April 21 comment: Alejandro is on PTO until April 28 with sole access to the cost simulation tooling, so production validation is blocked until he returns.
OE 4: Read comments on ENG-230 using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_invoice_attachment_safety"). Discovers Dmitri's April 18 comment: race condition found in Lena's PR #347, can't ship as-is. Discovers Lena's April 19 acknowledgment: reworking concurrency model, ready by the end of next week.
OE 5: Search Slack #engineering for ENG-220 discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "hash" or "duplicate" or "ENG-220") or conversations_history (channel: "C003"). Finds Dmitri's April 14 announcement that hash MVP is done (sounds complete), Lena's thread reply about integration (sounds like full integration), then Dmitri's standalone clarification that PDF similarity scorer is still needed. Also finds Anh's misleading April 22 "all clear" claiming remediation is wrapped up, contradicted by Dmitri's thread reply that hash matching is only half of ENG-220, and Lena's invoice PR got bounced.
OE 6: Search Slack #engineering for CloudWatch discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "CloudWatch" or "cost alerting" or "ENG-225"). Finds Anh's April 12 staging deploy message and Anh's April 20 claim that the namespace fix is deployed to production.
OE 7: Search emails for CloudWatch verification using search_emails (query: "CloudWatch" or "cost alert" or "test spike"). Finds Alejandro's April 20 email reporting that a simulated cost spike didn't trigger the alert, which contradicts Anh's Slack claim and reveals production validation was never confirmed after the fix.
OE 8: Search emails for ENG-230 / PR #347 / invoice safety updates using search_emails (query: "PR #347" or "concurrency" or "invoice" or "Lena"). Finds Lena's April 25 email to Dmitri (cc Samira): the concurrency rework is done, each validation is scoped into its own buffer context, swap scenario tests added, PR #347 is re-submitted and ready for re-review.
OE 9: Search Slack #engineering for ENG-230 discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "PR #347" or "ENG-230"). Finds Lena's April 16 PR #347 announcement, Marcus's urgency reply, and Lena's April 19 acknowledgment of the race condition.
OE 10: Search emails for the Mosaic auto-approval incident using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "auto-approved" or "Emeka"). Finds Emeka's April 26 email reporting three Mosaic employees got stipend claims auto-approved: Lena Kowalski ($380 headphones), Andre Mitchell ($650 standing desk converter), and Andre Mitchell ($125 webcam).
OE 11: Search Airtable for Mosaic stipend records using airtable_search_records (table: "tblStipends00001", field: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics") or airtable_list_records. Finds three records dated April 22–23 with Approval Status "Approved" and Review Notes "Auto-approved by ExpenseBot", confirms the incident is real despite auto-decisions being disabled since March 18.
OE 12: Add a comment on each ticket using linear_create_comment explaining current status: ENG-220 is blocked on ENG-230 (PDF scorer can't start until invoice safety ships); ENG-225 is blocked on Alejandro's PTO (sole access to simulation tooling, back April 28); ENG-230 concurrency rework is done and PR #347 is re-submitted and awaiting Dmitri's re-review (per Lena's April 25 email). None should be closed.
OE 13: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", priority: urgent/P1) for the Mosaic auto-approval incident, three claims auto-approved despite the March 18 disable, categorizer appears still running.
OE 14: Post a status update in Slack #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel: "C003") covering the real status of all three remediation tickets (ENG-220 blocked on ENG-230, ENG-225 blocked on Alejandro's PTO, ENG-230 awaiting re-review after Lena's concurrency fix), and the new Mosaic auto-approval incident as a live P1 before Monday's sprint planning.
OE 15: Search emails for Anh's OOO notice using search_emails (query: "Anh"). Finds Anh's April 26 email to Samira saying she won't be in Monday due to a family emergency, and asking to be reached at her personal email anh_wins@yahoo.com.
OE 16: Send email to Anh's personal address using send_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: anh_wins@yahoo.com) letting her know that the full status update has been posted in #engineering and she should check the channel, with a summary of key findings (remediation ticket statuses and the Mosaic auto-approval incident).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
